---
title: "Fred Boehm"
subtitle: "Assistant Professor of Statistics"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/Boehm_Fred_2025_WEB.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/fboehm
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/fred-boehm-27992a37a/
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=k7PBnv4AAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: person-badge
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1644-5931
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:frederick.boehm@sdstate.edu
---

## Welcome

I am a statistician with training in medicine and public health. I work as an Assistant Professor of Statistics at South Dakota State University. I develop statistical methods for human genetics data, with an emphasis in genome-wide association studies and rare variant association studies. The high dimensionality of genetics data provides many statistical and computing challenges. I try to leverage Bayesian statistical methods, for example, to compute genetic risk predictors for chronic diseases. I enjoy collaborative data science research projects, and I'm always looking for new opportunities.



## Interests

- Statistical genetics and genomics
- High-dimensional data analysis
- Bayesian methods
- Collaborative data science
- Pedagogy research in statistics, data science, and mathematics
